title: grants — health probes
description: Health probes registered with Foundation's health aggregator (surfaced via `GET /api/health`). Grants is a critical-path module — a broken overlay resolver = broken authorization. Probes here have low tolerance for latency + errors.

probes.name: grants-registry-integrity
probes.description: Verifies that every model composing #[Grantable] is (a) registered in GrantableRegistry, (b) also composes HasResourceGrants + IsGrantable, (c) has a valid preflight_validator class if declared. Fails startup if the invariant breaks — a consumer module's boot ordering bug should not silently produce grants that can't be enforced.

probes.name: grants-permission-catalogue-reachable
probes.description: Verifies the access/rbac permissions table is reachable + non-empty. Every grant write needs to cross-reference against this table; if unreachable, all writes fail with PERMISSION_UNKNOWN. Critical because the grant module cannot function without this reference.

probes.name: grants-eval-cache-reachable
probes.description: Verifies the L2 Redis evaluation cache is reachable. Non-critical because the resolver falls back to DB reads if L2 is unavailable — slower but correct.

probes.name: grants-overlay-resolver-latency
probes.description: Runs a synthetic GrantOverlayResolver::resolve() with a warm test-tenant + test-subject. Fails when p95 latency > 25ms over the last 60s. Non-critical (informational) — this is the canary for the SLO.

probes.name: grants-jobs-not-stalled
probes.description: Reads depth + oldest-job age for the `grants` job queue class (retention + notifications + bulk). Fails when depth > 5000 pending OR oldest job > 30 minutes on `retention` OR > 5 minutes on `notifications`. Stalled queues = grants stop invalidating cache = stale ACL decisions.

probes.name: grants-schedule-registered
probes.description: Boot-time. Verifies the six scheduled entries (purge-expired hourly, notify-expiring daily, purge-revoked daily, reconcile-orphaned dry-run + real weekly, review-flags monthly) are registered on the scheduler. Missing entries = grants never expire = permanent unintended access.

probes.name: grants-orphaned-grants-count
probes.description: Emits count of grants whose resource_id no longer exists. Fed by the weekly dry-run reconciliation. Non-critical but alerts fire above threshold (default 10 orphaned grants tenant-wide — anything higher signals cascade-delete bugs elsewhere).
